note:
YouTube取消了将近3000个频道和帐户，这些帐户和帐户被视为中国和俄罗斯国家独立影响力行动的一部分。
 根据Google威胁分析小组总监Shane Huntley的说法，绝大多数工作涉及删除2946个YouTube频道，这些频道被认为是中国影响力活动的一部分。
 他在2021年第一季度的TAG公告中解释说：“这些频道大多上传了中文的有关音乐，娱乐和生活方式的垃圾内容，其中一小部分上传了中文和英文的内容，涉及美国对COVID-19的回应以及美国政治的发展部门。”
 TAG到本季度为止的其余大部分工作都集中在俄罗斯。
 它包括终止三个YouTube频道，两个帐户和一个移动开发者帐户，作为竞选活动的一部分，该运动上传了反对派领导人Alexei Navalny举行的美国时事和政治集会的内容。
 删除了另外两个频道和一个广告帐户，用于上传有关阿富汗，亚美尼亚和乌克兰的历史事件的内容，而另外五个频道则发布了有关吞并克里米亚和叙利亚内战的视频。
 删除了另一个频道，用于上传有关乌克兰时事的内容，这被视为协调影响力行动的一部分。 
所有这些频道和帐户都以俄语播出内容，突显出克里姆林宫试图影响舆论的尝试不仅关注地缘政治敌人，还关注其本国人民。  普京政权特别担心受欢迎的政治家纳瓦尼（Navalny）的影响。纳瓦尼（Navalny）在一次明显的国家支持的暗杀企图中幸存下来，并返回祖国面对他的酷刑，他最近因被控最高罪名入狱。  Google还针对巴西，摩洛哥，吉尔吉斯斯坦，埃及和乌克兰的政府及其代理人的少数渠道和帐户采取了行动，这些渠道和帐户被认为是协调影响力行动的一部分。
note_en:
YouTube has taken down almost 3000 channels and accounts deemed to part of separate Chinese and Russian state coordinated influence operations.
 The vast majority of the work involved the removal of 2946 YouTube channels deemed to be part of a Chinese influence operation, according to Google’s Threat Analysis Group director, Shane Huntley.
 “These channels mostly uploaded spammy content in Chinese about music, entertainment and lifestyle,” he explained in a TAG bulletin for Q1 2021. “A very small subset uploaded content in Chinese and English about the US response to COVID-19 and growing US political divisions.”
 Much of the rest of the TAG’s work so far this quarter has been focused on Russia.
 It includes the termination of three YouTube channels, two accounts and one mobile developer account as part of campaigns which uploaded content on US current events and political rallies held by opposition leader Alexei Navalny.
 A further two channels and one advertising account were removed for uploading content about historical events in Afghanistan, Armenia and Ukraine, while five more channels published videos on the annexation of Crimea and the Syrian civil war.
 Another channel was taken down for uploading content about current events in Ukraine, deemed to be part of coordinated influence operations.
 All of these channels and accounts broadcast content in Russian, highlighting that the Kremlin’s attempts to influence public opinion focus not only on geopolitical foes but also its own people.
 The Putin regime is particularly anxious about the influence of popular politician Navalny, who was recently jailed on trumped up charges after surviving an apparent state-backed assassination attempt, and returning to the motherland to face his tormenter.
 Google also took action against a handful of channels and accounts run by governments and their proxies in Brazil, Morocco, Kyrgyzstan, Egypt and Ukraine which were deemed to be part of coordinated influence operations.
title: YouTube取消了将近3,000个频道和帐户，这些频道和帐户是中国国家协调影响力行动的一部分
title_en: YouTube takes down nearly 3,000 channels and accounts that were part of Chinese state coordinated influence operation

This is an alphabet inspired by Conway's Game of Life, a  cellular automaton created by John H. Conway in 1970. The  'game' involves an orthogonal grid, with 'live' cells and  'dead' cells. The grid changes over time - live cells can  die off (starvation or overpopulation), while dead cells can  be born/reborn (reproduction). An example is shown, using  a pixel form of the letter "G" and morphing it over 3 iterations.  (Live cells are black, dead cells are white.)
    The alphabet was generated by morphing each English letter,  plus 0-9, individually in Life (3 iterations each). The punctuation  marks are based on common stable ('still life') cell groups.
                "A 'live' cell dies if it has more than 3 live neighbors, or less than 2. A 'dead' cell is repopulated if it has exactly 3 live neighbors."

Gforth是GNU项目的Forth实现（当前版本0.7.3，请查看用户手册）。可以在GNU的Gforth目录中找到源代码发行版，还可以找到流行平台（例如Windows，GNU / Linux等）的源代码和二进制发行版。可以在Gforth的家中找到，也可以在Savannah的git存储库中找到开发版本的快照。阅读Gforth快照手册中有关快照的更新文档。
 Gforth使用GCC编译快速直接或间接线程的Forth； Gforth完全符合ANS FORTH。 Gforth的作者是Anton Ertl，Bernd Paysan，Jens Wilke，Neal Crook，DavidKühling等。
 Gforth项目的目标是为ANSIForth开发标准模型。这可以分为几个子目标：
 它应该成为标准，即被广泛接受和使用。这个目标是最困难的。
  强大的。它应该提供今天认为必要的所有东西，甚至还包括那些尚未被认为必要的东西。
 有一个面向对象的程序包，几乎用普通的ANS Forth（现在是Gforth发行版的一部分）编写。
  amd64 gdb反汇编程序可解决gcc 4.6和4.7问题（换行符和超级指令）的语法更改（自动检测）解决方法 
要求：：在某些平台上，运行时需要libtool和gcc（用于libcc C接口）和gdb（用于反汇编程序（SEE））。
                                 ＆＃39;现在只处理一个（x）字符：＆＃39; ab不再被接受，＆＃39; a＆＃39;现在产生（十进制）97
        16位和32位存储器访问：UW @ UL @ SW @ SL @ W！ L！ W @ L @ W L
 添加了对基于ffcall库构建的C接口的可选支持（比旧的库更可移植，功能更强大，但仍未记录）。要使用它，必须在构建Gforth之前安装ffcall库（请参阅INSTALL）。
  现在，Gforth-fast使用静态超级指令（某些平台上有一定的加速）。通常，这是透明的（除了加速之外），但是有许多用于控制静态超级指令生成的命令行选项。
  现在支持长字名称（在32位系统上为512MB）（更改为标头格式）。
 新的线程代码执行方法：以原始语言为中心（允许以下操作），混合直接/间接线程（更轻松的可移植性）以及动态超级指令（Athlon上的典型加速：因子2）。新引擎gforth-itc用于处理一些潜在的向后兼容性问题（请参见手册中的“直接或间接线程化”）。 
支持它们的OS上的大文件支持（即，在32位计算机上具有2GB以上的文件）。
    将prims2x.fs通用化为Vmgen（请参见README.vmgen等）；在prims中使用了新功能（例如，自动处理返回堆栈和指令流）。
 现在有两种引擎：快速引擎（gforth-fast）与早期版本中的gforth至少一样快；调试引擎（gforth）支持信号的精确回溯（例如，非法内存访问），但速度要慢1-2倍。
 现在，默认情况下，块文件从块0开始（而不是块1）。如果周围有块文件，请添加1024字节以进行转换，或者执行1 OFFSET！建立旧的行为。
 Gforth现在不会在阅读时将换行符转换为LF。而是，READ-LINE现在将LF，CR和CRLF解释为换行符。输出上的换行符采用操作系统喜欢的格式。

您可以使用＆＃34; gforthmi-应用程序...＆＃34;生成一个映像，该映像在直接调用时（而不是通过gforth -i）处理整个命令行。

note_en:
Gforth is the Forth implementation of the GNU project( Current release 0.7.3, havea look to the User Manual). Sourcedistributions can be found on GNU's Gforth directory, source and binarydistributions for popular platforms such as Windows, GNU/Linux,etc. can be found in Home of Gforth, as well as snapshotsof the  development versionin the git repository on Savannah. Read the updated documentation for thesnapshots in  Gforth's snapshot manual.
 Gforth uses GCC to compile a fast direct or indirect threaded Forth;Gforth is fully ANS FORTH compliant. Authors of Gforth are  Anton Ertl,  Bernd Paysan,  Jens Wilke, Neal Crook, David Kühling and others.
 The goal of the Gforth Project is to develop a standard model for ANSIForth. This can be split into several subgoals:
 It should become standard, i.e. widely accepted and used. This goalis the most difficult one.
  Powerful. It should provide for all the things that are considerednecessary today and even some that are not yet considered necessary.
 There's an  object oriented package, written in almost plain ANS Forth (now part of Gforth's distribution).
  amd64 gdb disassembler works with syntax change (autodetected) workaround for gcc 4.6 and 4.7 problems (newline and superinstructions)
 Requirements:: At run-time requires libtool and gcc (for the libcc C interface) and gdb (for the disassembler (SEE)) on some platforms.
                                 ' now only handles a single (x)char: 'ab is no longer accepted, 'a' now produces (decimal) 97
        16-bit and 32-bit memory acces: UW@ UL@ SW@ SL@ W! L! W@ L@  W L
 Added optional support for a C interface built on the ffcall libraries (more portable and powerful than the old one, but still not documented). To use it, the ffcall libraries have to be installed before building Gforth (see INSTALL).
  Gforth-fast now uses static superinstructions (some speedup on some platforms); generally this is transparent (apart from the speedup), but there are lots of command-line options for controlling the static superinstruction generation.
  Long word names (512MB on 32-bit systems) are now supported (change to the header format).
 New threaded code execution method: primitive-centric (allows the following), hybrid direct/indirect threaded (easier portability), with dynamic superinstructions (typical speedup on Athlon: factor 2). New engine gforth-itc for dealing with some potential backwards-compatibility problems (see "Direct or Indirect Threaded?" in the manual).
  Large file support on OSs that support them (i.e., files with more than 2GB on 32-bit machines).
    Generalized prims2x.fs into Vmgen (see README.vmgen etc.); used the new capabilities in prims (e.g., automatic handling of the return stack and instruction stream).
 There are now two engines: the fast engine (gforth-fast) is atleast as fast as gforth in earlier releases; the debugging engine(gforth) supports precise backtracing for signals (e.g., illegalmemory access), but is slower by a factor of 1-2.
 Block files now start at block 0 by default (instead of block 1). If you have block files around, prepend 1024 bytes to convert them, or do a  1 OFFSET ! to establish the old behaviour.
 Gforth now does not translate newlines to LFs on reading. Instead,  READ-LINE now interprets LF, CR, and CRLF as newlines. Newlines on output are in the OSs favourite format.
  On platforms supporting the Unix 98 SA_SIGINFO semantics, you get more precise error reports for SIGSEGV and SIGFPE (e.g., "stack underflow" instead of "Invalid memory address").
 Gforth now produces exit code 1 if there is an error (i.e., an uncaught  THROW) in batch processing.
 You can use "  gforthmi --application ..." to build an image thatprocesses the whole command-line when invoked directly (instead ofthrough  gforth -i).
      Significant extensions to the manual (added an introduction,among other things), many of them due to a new team member: NealCrook.
 Added assemblers and disassemblers for 386, Alpha, MIPS (thanks tocontributions by Andrew McKewan, Bernd Thallner, and ChristianPirker). Contributions of assemblers and disassemblers for otherarchitectures are welcome.
 Path handling: "." at the start of the path represents the directorythe nearest enclosing file resides in (if there is none: the workingdirectory). "~+" indicates the working directory. The default path nowhas "." in front.
  Now runs on IRIX (and other MIPS-based systems withoutlinker-flag -d). Direct threading now works on PowerPC (20% speedup on604e). Better support for m68k (thanks to Andreas Schwab and JorgeAcereda). It is possible to create executables that contain the image(for non-OS systems).
 Added a lot of embedded control (EC) stuff. Supported controllersand small CPUs are Siemens C16x, 8086, 6502, Mixed-Mode's FPGA MISC,Bernd Paysan's 4stack processor. Not finished: ShBoom alias PSC1000,H8, AVR.
  Renamed  F0 to  FP0(avoids unexpected behaviour in hex), added aliases  SP0,  RP0,  LP0(recommended for future use) for  S0,  R0,  L0.
 Renamed  PARSE-WORD into  SWORD( PARSE-WORD is used with the meaning of  NAME in OpenBoot anddpans6 A.6.2.2008)
  Reorganized files: added directories and reorganized many files into them; renamed files into 8.3 format to work with completely broken systems (but there are again some files that won't work there).
  Various changes without log information only known as mega-patches. Cross compiler now also supports compilation only for undefined or forward referenced words. Plugins to support some native code generation (for PSC1000).
        Created 21may1997. Last modified: 10nov2015 by   Bernd Paysan

note:
考古学家在阿拉斯加的三个史前爱斯基摩遗址发现了威尼斯玻璃贸易珠。在没有跨大西洋交流的情况下，这些文物从欧洲到阿拉斯加西北部的最可能路线是穿越欧亚大陆和白令海峡。这是有记录的第一个实例，表明西欧半球的史前遗址中存在不可逾越的欧洲材料，这是由于穿越欧亚大陆的陆路运输的结果。
  考古学家经常在整个加勒比海，中美洲和北美洲东部海岸以及东部大湖地区的1550年至1750年的考古遗址中发现“贸易珠”。
 欧洲人和其他人使用土著文化中不存在的技术制造了玻璃珠。
 探险者将他们带去与他们遇到的土著人进行贸易。荷兰人彼得·米努伊特（Peter Minuit）在1626年购买曼哈顿岛的交易中加入了贸易珠。
 来自阿拉斯加大学北方博物馆的麦克·库恩斯博士和土地管理局考古学家罗宾·米尔斯博士在普尼伊克角和阿拉斯加的另外两个考古现场发现了至少十个玻璃贸易珠。
 “ Punyik Point距离布鲁克斯山脉的大陆分界线仅一英里，今天却无人居住。这是几代内陆爱斯基摩人的季节性营地。”
 “ Punyik Point处在从白令海到北冰洋的古老贸易路线上，可能是一个在秋天和春天迁徙的驯鹿驯鹿的可靠地方。” 
“而且，如果由于某种原因，驯鹿没有从你那里迁移过，那么Punyik Point拥有极好的湖鳟鱼和大型灌木柳树斑。”
 研究人员发现，玻璃珠和在珠子附近发现的阿拉斯加麻线和木炭的放射性碳定年一起，在1440至1480年之间的某个时候到达了Punyik Point。
  “珠子是如何从威尼斯的运河流向布鲁克斯山脉的高原的？”他们说。
 “在1400年代，威尼斯城邦的工匠与整个亚洲的人们进行贸易。珠子可能是沿着马拉的马车沿着丝绸之路向东向中国移动的。”
 “从那里，这些早期的威尼斯珠子进入了土著腹地，并有一些移到了远东。”
 “经过那段伟大的旅程，商人可能将珠子塞进了白令海西岸的皮艇中。然后，他蘸了桨，进入了今天的阿拉斯加新世界。白令海峡最窄处的渡口大约有84公里（52英里）的公海。”
 科学家认为，在阿拉斯加发现的珠子可能到达了一个古老的交易中心，即Shashalik，位于今天的科兹布埃以北，就在诺塔克以西。 
他们说：“从那里步行的人，也许是带着几只狗旅行，将他们带到了布鲁克斯山脉的深处。”  “ Punyik Point的某人可能会把一条异国情调的蓝色珠子串在一条项链上，当他们走开时，它们会丢失或留下。”  “微小的蓝色球体在北极圈以北的地下房屋入口处休息了几个世纪，等待被发现。”  迈克尔·L·昆兹＆amp; 罗宾·米尔斯（Robin O. Mills）。 北极阿拉斯加的威尼斯玻璃贸易珠在哥伦比亚前的存在。 《美国古代》，于2021年1月20日在线发布； doi：10.1017 / aaq.2020.100
note_en:
Archaeologists have found Venetian glass trade beads at three prehistoric Eskimo sites in Alaska. In the absence of trans-Atlantic communication, the most likely route these artifacts traveled from Europe to northwestern Alaska is across Eurasia and over the Bering Strait. This is the first documented instance of the presence of indubitable European materials in prehistoric sites in the western hemisphere as the result of overland transport across the Eurasian continent.
  Archaeologists often find ‘ trade beads’ at archaeological sites dating between 1550 and 1750 CE throughout the Caribbean, the eastern coast of Central and North America, and the eastern Great Lakes region.
 Europeans and others created glass beads using technology that didn’t exist in Native cultures.
 Explorers carried them to trade with aboriginal people they encountered. Dutchman Peter Minuit  included trade beads in his deal for Manhattan Island in 1626.
 Dr. Mike Kunz from the University of Alaska Museum of the North and Bureau of Land Management archaeologist Dr. Robin Mills found at least ten glass trade beads at Punyik Point and two other archaeological sites in Alaska.
 “Punyik Point, a mile from the Continental Divide in the Brooks Range, is unoccupied today. It was a seasonal camp for generations of inland Eskimos,” Dr. Kunz said.
 “Punyik Point was on ancient trade routes from the Bering Sea to the Arctic Ocean, and was probably a dependable place to hunt caribou as the animals moved in fall and spring.”
 “And, if for some reason the caribou didn’t migrate through where you were, Punyik Point had excellent lake trout and large shrub-willow patches.”
 Along with the radiocarbon dating of the Alaska twine and charcoal found near the beads, the researchers figured the glass beads arrived at Punyik Point sometime between 1440 and 1480.
  “How did the beads make their way from the canals of Venice to a plateau in the Brooks Range?” they said.
 “In the 1400s, craftsmen in the city-state of Venice traded with people throughout Asia. The beads might have traveled in a horse-drawn cart along the Silk Road eastward toward China.”
 “From there, these early Venetian beads found their way into the aboriginal hinterlands, with some moving to the Far East.”
 “After that great journey, a trader may have tucked the beads into his kayak on the western shore of the Bering Sea. He then dipped his paddle and made passage to the New World, today’s Alaska. The crossing of Bering Strait at its narrowest is about 84 km (52 miles) of open ocean.”
 The scientists think the beads found in Alaska probably arrived at an ancient trading center called Shashalik, north of today’s Kotzebue and just west of Noatak.
 “From there, people on foot, maybe traveling with a few dogs, carried them deep into the Brooks Range,” they said.
 “Someone at Punyik Point might have strung the exotic blue beads in a necklace, which they lost or left behind as they walked away.”
 “The tiny blue spheres rested for centuries at the entrance to an underground house north of the Arctic Circle, waiting to be found.”
   Michael L. Kunz & Robin O. Mills. A Precolumbian Presence of Venetian Glass Trade Beads in Arctic Alaska.  American Antiquity, published online January 20, 2021; doi: 10.1017/aaq.2020.100
title: 在北极阿拉斯加发现威尼斯玻璃珠早于哥伦布
title_en: Venetian Glass Beads Found in Arctic Alaska Predate Arrival of Columbus

note:
Kristen Berman是行为研究和设计咨询公司Irrational Labs的联合创始人兼首席执行官。
 Evelyn Gosnell是行为研究和设计咨询公司Irrational Labs的董事总经理。
 Richard Mathera是行为研究和设计咨询公司Irrational Labs的董事总经理。
  如果我们能及时传送回去，重新启动Facebook，Twitter和TikTok等社交媒体平台，以期从一开始就尽量减少错误信息和阴谋论的传播……它们的面貌如何？
 这不是学术活动。了解这些根本原因可以帮助我们针对当前和将来的平台制定更好的预防措施。
   作为硅谷领先的行为科学公司之一，我们已经帮助Google，Lyft等品牌了解与产品设计相关的人为决策。我们最近与TikTok合作，设计了一系列新的提示（本周发布），以帮助阻止潜在错误信息在其平台上的传播。
  干预成功地将标记内容的份额减少了24％。尽管TikTok在平台之间是独一无二的，但我们在那里获得的经验教训已帮助人们塑造了社交媒体Redux的外观。 
在减少未经证实的内容的视图方面，我们可以采取比标签或提示更大的措施。
 在我们与TikTok共同发起的实验中，人们在两周内平均观看了1.5个标记的视频。然而，在我们的定性研究中，许多用户说他们是在TikTok上玩耍的。他们不想看到任何被标记的视频。在最近的财报电话会议上，马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）还谈到了Facebook用户对超级党派内容的厌倦。
 我们建议给人们一个“选择退出标记内容”的选项-完全从他们的提要中删除这些内容。为了使其成为一个真正的选择，这种退出选择必须突出，而不是埋葬在用户必须寻找的地方。我们建议新用户直接将其放入注册流程中，并为现有用户添加应用内提示。
  虚假新闻在社交媒体上的传播速度是真实新闻的六倍，这是有原因的：具有争议性，戏剧性或两极分化的信息更有可能引起我们的注意。而且，当设计算法以最大程度地提高用户参与度和在应用程序上花费的时间时，这种内容将比更周到的，经过深思熟虑的内容更受青睐。
 基于广告的业务模型是问题的核心。这就是为什么在错误信息和两极分化方面取得进展如此困难的原因。负责调查该问题的一个内部Facebook团队发现，“我们的算法利用了人脑对分裂的吸引力。”但是，解决该问题的项目和拟议工作被高级管理人员所困扰。
 本质上，这是一个经典的激励问题。如果定义“成功”的业务指标不再取决于最大化现场参与度/时间，那么一切都会改变。极化的内容将不再需要被偏爱，并且更周到的论述将能够浮出水面。
  错误信息传播的主要部分是边缘化和孤独感。从根本上讲，人类是社交动物，看起来像是一个群体中的一部分，而有党派的群体常常会提供这种接受和确认的感觉。 
因此，我们必须使人们更容易以其他方式找到其真实的部落和社区（与那些基于阴谋理论的人相比）。
 马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）说，他与Facebook的最终目标是建立人与人之间的联系。公平地说，至少在表面上，Facebook已经通过许多方式做到了这一点。但是我们应该更深入。以下是一些方法：
 我们可以设计出更积极的一对一交流，事实证明这可以增加幸福感。我们还可以微调离线连接。想象有两个朋友正在Facebook Messenger上聊天或通过帖子评论聊天。当他们住在同一个城市时，提示亲自见面怎么样（当然是COVID后）？或者，如果他们不在同一个城市，请轻推以拨打电话或视频。
 在他们不是朋友并且互动更具争议的情况下，平台不仅可以突出对方的个性，而且可以分享彼此的共同点。想象一下一个提示，其中显示了当您与某人在线“大喊”时，您与该人的共同之处。
 平台还应该禁止匿名帐户，或者至少鼓励使用真实姓名。俱乐部会所对此有很好的规范设定：在入职流程中，他们说：“我们在这里使用真实姓名。”连接是基于我们正在与真实的人互动的想法。匿名混淆了这一点。
  我们应该使人们更容易摆脱算法上的兔子洞。 YouTube因其漏洞而备受抨击，但所有社交媒体平台都面临这一挑战。点击视频后，系统会向您显示喜欢的视频。有时这可能会有所帮助（获取完美的“操作方法”视频有时需要进行搜索），但是对于错误消息，这是一场致命的游行。一个在平地上的视频会引出另一个以及其他阴谋论。我们需要帮助人们摆脱算法的命运。
  现在越来越多的人从社交媒体获取新闻，而这样做的人则不太可能正确了解重要问题。依靠社交媒体作为信息源的趋势很可能会继续。 
因此，社交媒体公司处于独特的权力地位，有责任深入思考他们在减少错误信息传播中所扮演的角色。 他们绝对应该像我们与TikTok团队一样，继续使用具有研究意义的解决方案进行实验和运行测试。  这项工作并不容易。 我们知道这种想法，但是在与TikTok团队合作之后，我们对此事有了更深的赞赏。 有许多聪明，好主意的人想要解决更大的问题。 我们非常希望在这里有集体的机会，可以更大胆和更有创意地思考如何减少错误信息，激发联系并增强我们的集体人性。
note_en:
Kristen Berman is the co-founder and CEO of  Irrational Labs, a behavioral research and design consultancy.
 Evelyn Gosnell is a managing director at  Irrational Labs, a behavioral research and design consultancy.
 Richard Mathera is a managing director at  Irrational Labs, a behavioral research and design consultancy.
  If we could teleport back in time to relaunch social media platforms like Facebook, Twitter and TikTok with the goal of minimizing the spread of misinformation and conspiracy theories from the outset … what would they look like?
 This is not an academic exercise. Understanding these root causes can help us develop better prevention measures for current and future platforms.
   As one of the Valley’s leading behavioral science firms, we’ve helped brands like Google, Lyft and others understand human decision-making as it relates to product design. We recently collaborated with TikTok to design a new series of prompts (launched this week) to help stop the spread of potential misinformation on its platform.
  The intervention successfully reduces shares of flagged content by 24%. While TikTok is unique amongst platforms, the lessons we learned there have helped shape ideas on what a social media redux could look like.
  We can take much bigger swings at reducing the views of unsubstantiated content than labels or prompts.
 In the experiment we launched together with TikTok, people saw an average of 1.5 flagged videos over a two-week period. Yet in our qualitative research, many users said they were on TikTok for fun; they didn’t want to see any flagged videos whatsoever. In a recent earnings call, Mark Zuckerberg also spoke of  Facebook users’ tiring of hyperpartisan content.
 We suggest giving people an “opt-out of flagged content” option — remove this content from their feeds entirely. To make this a true choice, this opt-out needs to be prominent, not buried somewhere users must seek it out. We suggest putting it directly in the sign-up flow for new users and adding an in-app prompt for existing users.
  There’s a reason  false news spreads six times faster on social media than real news: Information that’s controversial, dramatic or polarizing is far more likely to grab our attention. And when algorithms are designed to maximize engagement and time spent on an app, this kind of content is heavily favored over more thoughtful, deliberative content.
 The ad-based business model is at the core the problem; it’s why making progress on misinformation and polarization is so hard. One internal Facebook team tasked with looking into the issue found that, “ our algorithms exploit the human brain’s attraction to divisiveness.” But the project and proposed work to address the issues was nixed by senior executives.
 Essentially, this is a classic incentives problem. If business metrics that define “success” are no longer dependent on maximizing engagement/time on site, everything will change. Polarizing content will no longer need to be favored and more thoughtful discourse will be able to rise to the surface.
  A primary part of the spread of misinformation is  feeling marginalized and alone. Humans are fundamentally social creatures who look to be part of an in-group, and partisan groups frequently provide that sense of acceptance and validation.
 We must therefore make it easier for people to find their authentic tribes and communities in other ways (versus those that bond over conspiracy theories).
 Mark Zuckerberg says his ultimate goal with Facebook was to connect people. To be fair, in many ways Facebook has done that, at least on a surface level. But we should go deeper. Here are some ways:
 We can design for more active one-on-one communication,  which has been shown to increase well-being. We can also nudge offline connection. Imagine two friends are chatting on Facebook messenger or via comments on a post. How about a prompt to meet in person, when they live in the same city (post-COVID, of course)? Or if they’re not in the same city, a nudge to hop on a call or video.
 In the scenario where they’re not friends and the interaction is more contentious, platforms can play a role in highlighting not only the humanity of the other person, but things one shares in common with the other. Imagine a prompt that showed, as you’re “shouting” online with someone, everything you have in common with that person.
 Platforms should also disallow anonymous accounts, or at minimum encourage the use of real names. Clubhouse has good norm-setting on this: In the onboarding flow they say, “We use real names here.” Connection is based on the idea that we’re interacting with a real human. Anonymity obfuscates that.
  We should make it easy for people to get out of an algorithmic rabbit hole. YouTube has been under fire for its rabbit holes, but all social media platforms have this challenge. Once you click a video, you’re shown videos like it. This may help sometimes (getting to that perfect “how to” video sometimes requires a search), but for misinformation, this is a death march. One video on flat earth leads to another, as well as other conspiracy theories. We need to help people eject from their algorithmic destiny.
  More and more people now get their news from social media, and those who do  are less likely to be correctly informed about important issues. It’s likely that this trend of relying on social media as an information source will continue.
 Social media companies are thus in a unique position of power and have a responsibility to think deeply about the role they play in reducing the spread of misinformation. They should absolutely continue to experiment and run tests with research-informed solutions, as we did together with the TikTok team.
 This work isn’t easy. We knew that going in, but we have an even deeper appreciation for this fact after working with the TikTok team. There are many smart, well-intentioned people who want to solve for the greater good. We’re deeply hopeful about our collective opportunity here to think bigger and more creatively about how to reduce misinformation, inspire connection and strengthen our collective humanity all at the same time.
title: 减少错误信息在社交媒体上的传播：重整是什么样子？
title_en: Reducing the spread of misinformation on social media: What would a do-over look like?

美国司法部周三对三名涉嫌串谋从世界各地的银行和企业窃取和勒索超过13亿美元现金和加密货币的朝鲜间谍进行了公开起诉。
  起诉书是基于2018年针对朝鲜政府2014年对Sony Pictures Entertainment的网络攻击而对一名涉嫌黑客的指控，这是美国首次起诉平壤特工。
 此举表明朝鲜在一个主要出口受到联合国和美国制裁的国家中依靠金融网络盗窃来获取硬通货的程度，并通过自我施加的冠状病毒封锁进一步隔离了朝鲜。
  官员们还宣布，一名加拿大裔美国人对协助涉嫌的朝鲜黑客的洗钱活动表示认罪。
   国家安全助理总检察长约翰·德默斯（John Demers）说：“朝鲜的特工使用键盘而不是枪支，窃取加密货币的数字钱包而不是现金，是世界上主要的银行抢劫犯。”
 根据去年12月提交的起诉书，被告在朝鲜军事情报局侦察总局工作。该机构设有各种名称的黑客单位，包括Lazarus Group和APT38。
 其中一名被告朴金赫（Park Jin Hyok），也被控就2018年9月未公开的Sony黑客事件提出起诉。另外两名是John Chang Hyok和Kim Il。 
美国律师在洛杉矶的办公室和联邦调查局还获得了逮捕令，据称他们从纽约一家银行扣押了据称被黑客盗取的约190万美元的加密货币，该加密货币在两个加密货币交易所持有。官员说，这笔钱将退还银行。
   “北韩黑客的犯罪行为范围广泛且长期存在，他们犯下的罪行范围令人震惊，”加利福尼亚中央区代理美国检察官特雷西·威尔基森说。这些“是一个犯罪民族国家的行为，该民族不遗余力地进行了报复，并获得了资金来支持其政权。”
  检察官称，这种阴谋范围广泛，操作人员侵入银行和加密货币交易所，并于2017年5月制造了破坏性勒索软件病毒WannaCry。他们被指控从2018年3月至至少2020年9月开发了多种恶意加密货币应用程序。为黑客提供了进入受害者计算机的后门。
 检察官称，他们瞄准了加密货币交易所，2017年从一家斯洛文尼亚公司窃取了7500万美元，2018年从一家印尼公司窃取了2500万美元，8月从纽约一家银行窃取了1180万美元，其中黑客使用CryptoNeuro Trader应用程序作为后门。
   他们还针对美国国防承包商，能源，航空航天和技术公司以及国务院和五角大楼进行了“鱼叉式网络钓鱼”运动，以诱骗员工放弃让黑客进入其计算机的凭据。
 美国企业研究所（American Enterprise Institute）的经济学家尼古拉斯·埃伯哈特（Nicholas Eberhardt）表示，据称被盗的13亿美元将占朝鲜民用商品进口总额（主要是从中国进口）的几乎一半，该数字主要是从中国进口的。该数字是最新的估计数字。他说：“因此，这对朝鲜经济来说意义重大。”
note_en:
The Justice Department on Wednesday unsealed charges against three North Korean hacker spies accused of conspiring to steal and extort more than $1.3 billion in cash and cryptocurrency from banks and businesses around the world.
  The indictment builds upon 2018 charges brought against one of the alleged hackers in connection with the North Korean regime’s 2014 cyber attack on Sony Pictures Entertainment, marking the first time the United States charged a Pyongyang operative.
 The move shows the degree to which North Korea relies on financial cyber theft to obtain hard currency in a country whose main exports are under United Nations and U.S. sanctions, and that is further isolated by a self-imposed coronavirus blockade.
  Officials also announced that a Canadian-American citizen has pleaded guilty to serving as a money launderer who assisted the alleged North Korean hackers.
   “North Korea’s operatives, using keyboards rather than guns, stealing digital wallets of cryptocurrency instead of sacks of cash, are the world’s leading bank robbers,” Assistant Attorney General for National Security John Demers said.
 According to the indictment filed in December, the defendants work for the Reconnaissance General Bureau, North Korea’s military intelligence agency. The agency houses the hacking units known by various names, including Lazarus Group and APT38.
 One of the defendants, Park Jin Hyok, was also charged in a complaint about the Sony hack unsealed in September 2018. The other two are John Chang Hyok and Kim Il.
 The U.S. Attorney’s office in Los Angeles and the FBI also obtained warrants to seize about $1.9 million in cryptocurrency allegedly stolen by the hackers from a New York bank and that was held at two cryptocurrencey exchanges. The money will be returned to the bank, officials said.
   “The scope of the criminal conduct by the North Korean hackers was extensive and long-running and the range of crimes they have committed is staggering,” said Acting U.S. Attorney Tracy L. Wilkison for the Central District of California. These “are the acts of a criminal nation-state that has stopped at nothing to extract revenge and obtain money to prop up its regime.”
  The conspiracy ranged widely, prosecutors allege, with the operatives hacking into banks and crypto exchanges, and creating a destructive ransomware virus, WannaCry, in May 2017. They are accused of developing multiple malicious cryptocurrency applications from March 2018 through at least September 2020, which provided the hackers a backdoor into victims’ computers.
 They targeted cryptocurrency exchanges, stealing $75 million from a Slovenian company in 2017, $25 million from an Indonesian company in 2018 and $11.8 million from a bank in New York in August in which the hackers used the CryptoNeuro Trader application as a backdoor, prosecutors alleged.
   They also conducted “spear-phishing” campaigns targeting U.S. defense contractors and energy, aerospace and technology companies, as well as the State Department and Pentagon, to trick employees into giving up credential enabling the hackers’ entry into their computers.
 The $1.3 billion allegedly stolen would represent almost half of the total amount of North Korea’s civilian merchandise imports — mainly from China — in 2019, the most recent year for which estimates are available, said Nicholas Eberhardt an economist at the American Enterprise Institute. “So it’s a huge big deal for the North Korean economy,” he said.

投资者在“二级市场”交易中对Stripe的估值为1,150亿美元，私人公司股票在首次发行后即被出售。该估值比Stripe在2020年4月从包括安德森·霍洛维茨（Andreessen Horowitz），General Catalyst和Sequoia在内的风投公司筹集资金时获得的360亿美元高出三倍多。
 知情人士称，Stripe还计划进行新一轮的主要融资，估值超过1000亿美元。 Stripe的发言人拒绝置评。
 这家位于旧金山的公司由爱尔兰兄弟帕特里克（Patrick）和约翰·科里森（John Collison）于2010年创立。该公司帮助包括Peloton和加拿大电子商务平台Shopify在内的各种规模的企业接受在线支付，每笔交易都需付费。虽然Covid加快了消费者向在线购物的转变，但Stripe及其同行已经看到对其服务的需求激增。
 总部位于荷兰的Adyen是Stripe最接近的竞争对手，并将Netflix作为客户。过去一年来，该公司的股票上涨了150％，达到660亿美元。 PayPal的股价也上涨了同样多，并且通过其在2013年购买的Braintree付款处理服务与Stripe竞争。Stripe的业务规模可能与Adyen和Braintree的规模相似，投资研究公司的合伙人兼高级分析师Lisa Ellis估计莫菲特·内森（MoffettNathanson）。她说，但Stripe的增长速度可能更快，并且它与Shopify和亚马逊的合作伙伴关系使其具有竞争优势。
 Ellis说，Adyen，PayPal和Stripe共同构成了全球所有付款处理的10％。然而，与较老的，更成熟的处理业务（例如，位于佛罗里达州的FIS，位于威斯康星州的Fiserv，亚特兰大的Global Payments和摩根大通（JPMorgan Chase）相比，它们的市场规模很小，后者的市场份额合计为40％。 （全球交易的其余50％由本地公司和区域银行处理。）
 艾利斯（Ellis）估计，Stripe在2019年处理了200到2500亿美元的交易，并在2020年增长了约50％。她对Stripe的1,150亿美元估值怎么看？她说：“这就像现在所有私人估值对金融科技中的任何事物都是疯狂的一样。”但是鉴于他们的竞争地位，“这绝对不是疯子。”
  在过去的12个月中，金融科技的估值激增，例如Robinhood，租房者和房主的保险公司Lemonade，现在购买后付款的公司Affirm和在线账单支付平台Bill.com等公司的估值都上涨了三倍甚至更多。已有超过半数的公司已经上市或处于上市后期。 Stripe是传闻于2021年某个时候上市的众多金融科技公司之一。

note_en:
Investors are valuing Stripe at a $115 billion valuation in “secondary market” transactions, where shares of a private company’s stock are sold after they were first issued. That valuation is up more than threefold from the $36 billion Stripe fetched when it raised money in April 2020 from venture firms including Andreessen Horowitz, General Catalyst and Sequoia.
 Stripe is also planning a new primary funding round at a valuation north of $100 billion, according to a person familiar with the matter. A spokesperson from Stripe declined to comment.
 The San Francisco company was founded by Irish brothers Patrick and John Collison in 2010. It helps businesses small and large—including Peloton and Canadian ecommerce platform Shopify—accept online payments, taking a fee on every transaction. While Covid has accelerated consumers’ shift to online shopping, Stripe and its peers have seen demand for its services swell.
 Netherlands-based Adyen is Stripe’s closest competitor and counts Netflix as a customer. It has seen its stock jump 150% over the past year, reaching $66 billion. PayPal’s stock has risen just as much, and it competes with Stripe through its Braintree payment processing service that it bought in 2013. Stripe’s business is likely a similar size as Adyen’s and Braintree’s, estimates Lisa Ellis, a partner and senior analyst at investment research firm MoffettNathanson. But Stripe is likely growing faster, and its partnerships with Shopify and Amazon give it a competitive advantage, she says.
 Together, Adyen, PayPal and Stripe make up 10% of all payment processing globally, says Ellis. Yet they’re small compared with the older, more mature processing businesses like Florida-based FIS, Wisconsin-based Fiserv, Atlanta’s Global Payments and JPMorgan Chase, which have a combined 40% market share. (The remaining 50% of global transactions are processed by local companies and regional banks.)
 Ellis estimates that Stripe processed $200 - $250 billion in transactions in 2019 and grew roughly 50% in 2020. What does she think of a $115 billion valuation for Stripe? “It’s nuts in the same way that all private valuations are nuts right now for anything in fintech,” she says. But given their competitive position, “it’s not utterly insane.”
  Over the past 12 months, fintech valuations have ballooned, with companies like Robinhood, renters and homeowners’ insurer Lemonade, buy-now-pay-later company  Affirm and online bill payment platform  Bill.com seeing their values rise threefold or more. More than a half-dozen have gone public or are in the late stages of doing so. Stripe is among the many fintech companies rumored to be going public sometime in 2021.

防洪课很棒。缺乏令人失望的行话，但是技术很多。您知道，在我的第三个防波塑料篷布冲掉后，就像面对接缝并向下游折叠而不是顺流而入，这一切都是我想到的。但老实说，我要花上一百个沙袋才能学会将第一个铲子扔到袋子的边缘，以便在将下三个铲子扔到袋子的边缘之前，要与伴侣的手保持安全距离。在物理世界中移动的技术太多了。系上沙袋时，您是否会想到将其握住一个角并旋转，直到布料翻倍并变得易于捆扎？再说一次，在用一百个沙袋艰难地完成它之后，当我看到它时，我会立即意识到更好的方法。但是我不知道什么时候想到。班上一些有趣的事情：像我们一样无能和笨拙，沙袋飞速前进。观看机组人员几天的练习一定是令人惊讶的；像快动作电影，但不是。
 如果水流过堤坝，水会沸腾出另一面。煮沸清除不是问题。堤坝可以像几十年一样渗透甚至挖洞。如果煮沸器承载着物质，您可能会看到煮沸器周围有一个小火山，那么它会冲刷大堤的内部。直到堤坝倒塌为止
 那么如何停止脏stop呢？再说一次，我永远也不会想到这一点。我本来会装满一个沙袋然后塞住水煮的。但是，不，您永远不会停止沸腾；堤坝内的水将找到新的出口。取而代之的是，您在其周围放一圈沙袋，然后让水充满。增加沙袋环的高度，以减小堤坝两侧水域之间的水头差。当沸腾的水清澈时，请停止操作，然后再完全停止使用自来水。留一个小的溢洪道，以使水从沙袋环中排出。
 金矿开采日造成的环境破坏仍在造成麻烦。萨克拉曼多河的河床在其大部分时间内被抬高了几英尺。从水工开采下来的沙子上筑起的堤坝正在从底部被吞噬。有140年历史的决策是活跃的问题。
 课堂上的一件可怕的事：教授课程的人是守旧派工程师和维护人员。他们有肚子，可以驾驶重型机械，还开着玩笑。他们在使我印象深刻的事情上非常胜任，例如开车赌注或绑快速挂钩。他们务实，知道自己的风景，而且在很大程度上是没有想象力的。我绝对相信他们会在半夜的大雨中奔跑，以拯救我和我。那些家伙？他们对加利福尼亚州的新发展感到恐惧和愤怒。他们说，“在那个弯头的堤防每次都会失败。它永远不会保留，但现在后面有六千栋新房子。” “我不知道我们要怎么办，‘因为上次我们可以在那儿开一条溢洪道，但是现在……所有的新房子……。” “我们不知道该在哪里提供保护所有这些地方的机械，我不知道。但是，如果不再是农田，我们将不得不这样做。”他们不是民主党人，也不是环保主义者。但是他们讨厌堤坝后面的蔓延，也让我感到害怕。
note_en:
Flood fighting class was great. There was a disappointing lack of jargon, but a ton of technique. You know, it was all technique that would have occurred to me after my third wave protection plastic tarp washed away, like facing the seams and folds downstream instead of into the current. But honestly, it would have taken me the first hundred sandbags to learn to throw the first shovelful on the lip of the bag to anchor it before throwing the next three shovelfuls in, from a safe distance from your partner’s hand. There is so much technique to moving in the physical world. When you tie off a sandbag, would you have thought to hold it by one corner and spin it until the cloth doubles over and becomes easy to tie? Again, after doing it the hard way for a hundred sandbags, I would have immediately recognized the better way when I saw it. But I don’t know when I would have thought of it.  Some interesting things from the class:   Incompetent and bungling as we were, sandbagging goes shockingly fast. It must be astonishing to watch a crew with a few days’ practice; like fast motion film, but not.
 If water is channeling through a levee, it will come out the other side in a boil. A boil running clear is not a problem. Levees can seep or even tunnel like that for decades. If the boil is carrying material, which you might see as a little volcano building around the boil, it is scouring the inside of the levee. That’ll grow until the levee collapses.
 So how do you stop a dirty boil? Again, I would never have thought of this; I would have filled a sandbag and plugged the boil. But no, you never stop a running boil; the water inside the levee will find a new exit. Instead, you put a ring of sandbags around it and let the water fill that. Increase the height of your sandbag ring to decrease the difference in head between waters on the two sides of the levee. Stop when the water from the boil runs clear but before you stop the running water entirely. Leave a small spillway for water to drain from your sandbag ring.
 Environmental damage from the gold mining days is still causing trouble. The bed of the Sacramento River was raised several feet for much of its length. Levees built on sands washed down from hydraulic mining are being eaten from the bottom. 140 year old decisions are active problems.
 A scary thing from the class:  The guys who taught the class are old school engineers and maintenance guys. They have bellies and they can drive heavy machinery and they make jokes about the enviros. They are tremendously competent at things that impress me, like driving stakes or tying fast hitches. They are pragmatic and know their landscapes and they are largely unimaginative. I absolutely trust them to run crews in a cold rain in the middle of the night, there to save me and mine. Those guys? They are scared and angry at the new development going up in California. They say things like “That levee at that bend is gonna fail everytime. It’ll never hold, but now there’re six thousand new houses behind it.” “I don’t know what we’re gonna do, ‘cause last time we could break out a spillway there, but now… all the new houses… .” “Where we’re gonna get the machinery to protect all those places at once, I don’t know. But we’re gonna have to, if it isn’t farmland any more.” They are not Democrats and they are not environmentalists. But they  hate the sprawl behind levees and they make me scared too.

note:
Nx是Elixir的多维张量库，具有对CPU / GPU的多阶段编译。它的高级功能是：
 类型化的多维张量，其中的张量可以是无符号整数（8、16、32、64的大小），有符号整数（8、16、32、64的大小），浮点数（32、64的大小）和脑浮点数（16的大小） ）;
 命名张量，使开发人员可以为每个维度命名，从而使代码库更具可读性，并且不易出错。
 自动分化，也称为autograd。梯度函数提供反向模式微分，可用于仿真，训练概率模型等；
 张量后端，使主要的Nx API可以用于处理二进制张量，GPU支持的张量，稀疏矩阵等；
 数值定义（称为defn）为多个目标（例如高度专业的CPU代码或GPU）提供张量操作的多阶段编译。使用您选择的编译器，编译可以提前进行（AOT）或及时进行（JIT）。
 您可以在问题跟踪器中找到计划的增强功能。如果您需要一项特定功能来向前迈进，请随时告知我们并提供反馈。 
Nx的吉祥物是Numbat，它是原产于澳大利亚南部的有袋动物。不幸的是，Numbat濒临灭绝，估计还不到1000。如果您喜欢这个项目，可以考虑捐赠Numbat保护工作，例如Numbat项目和澳大利亚野生动物保护组织。
 对于Python开发人员而言，Nx当前主要从Numpy和JAX汲取灵感，但打包到一个统一的库中。
    在Elixir的任何社区空间（例如Elixir论坛和elixir-lang.org侧边栏中列出的其他频道）中，也欢迎对Nx进行讨论。
  为了使用Nx，您将需要安装Elixir。然后通过混合构建工具创建一个Elixir项目：
  然后，您可以在mix.exs中添加Nx作为依赖项。目前，在我们开发第一个版本时，您将不得不使用Git依赖项：
      iex＆gt; t = Nx。张量（[[1，2]，[3，4]] iex＆gt; Nx。除法（Nx.exp（t），Nx.sum（Nx.exp（t）））＃Nx.Tensor＆lt; f64 [2] [2] [[0.03205860328008499，0.08714431874203257]，[0.23688281808991013，0.6439142598879722]]＆gt;
  默认情况下，Nx使用纯Elixir代码。由于Elixir是一种功能性且不变的语言，因此上述每个操作都会复制张量，这是非常低效的。 
但是，Nx还带有称为defn的数字定义，这是为数字计算量身定制的Elixir的子集。例如，它会覆盖Elixir的默认运算符，因此它们可感知张量：
  defn支持多个编译器后端，这些后端可以编译所述函数以在CPU或GPU中运行。例如，使用EXLA编译器，该编译器提供了对Google XLA的绑定：
  调用softmax后，Nx.Defn将调用EXLA来生成针对张量类型和形状量身定制的即时且高度专业的代码编译版本。通过传递client：：cuda或client：：rocm，可以为GPU编译代码。作为参考，以下是使用一百万个随机浮点值的张量调用时上述函数的一些基准：
 名称ips平均偏差中位数99th％xla gpu f32保持15308.14 0.0653 ms±29.01％0.0638 ms 0.0758 msxla gpu f64保持4550.59 0.22 ms±7.54％0.22 ms 0.33 msxla cpu f32 434.21 2.30 ms±7.54％2.26 ms 2.69 msxla gpu f32 398.45 2.51 ms±2.28％2.50 ms 2.69 msxla gpu f64 190.27 5.26 ms±2.16％5.23 ms 5.56 msxla cpu f64 168.25 5.94 ms±5.64％5.88 ms 7.35 mselixir f32 3.22 311.01 ms±1.88％309.69 ms 340.27 mselixir f64 3.11 321.70 ms±1.44％ 322.10 ms 328.98 ms比较：xla gpu f32保持15308.14xla gpu f64保持4550.59-慢3.36x +0.154 msxla cpu f32 434.21-慢35.26x +2.24 msxla gpu f32 398.45-38.42x慢+2.44 msxla gpu f32 -2.464 xxla gpu f64 46。 5.19 msxla cpu f64 168.25-慢90.98x +5.88 mselixir f32 3.22-4760.93x慢+310.94 mselixir f64 3.11-4924.56x慢+321.63 ms
  defn依靠一种称为多阶段编程的技术，该技术建立在Elixir功能和元编程功能的基础上：我们将Elixir代码转换为发出AST的代码，然后将其转换为在CPU / GPU上运行。最终，defn编译器是可插入的，这意味着开发人员可以为不同的张量编译器技术实现绑定并选择最合适的技术。
 defn中支持Elixir的许多功能，例如管道运算符，别名，条件，模式匹配等。路线图上还包含其他功能，例如循环和就地更新。 defn还支持转换，该转换允许在运行时转换数字定义。通过grad函数自动进行区分是转换的一个示例。

note_en:
Nx is a multi-dimensional tensors library for Elixir with multi-staged compilation to the CPU/GPU. Its high-level features are:
 Typed multi-dimensional tensors, where the tensors can be unsigned integers (sizes 8, 16, 32, 64), signed integers (sizes 8, 16, 32, 64), floats (sizes 32, 64) and brain floats (sizes 16);
 Named tensors, allowing developers to give names to each dimension, leading to more readable and less error prone codebases;
 Automatic differentiation, also known as autograd. The  grad function provides reverse-mode differentiation, useful for simulations, training probabilistic models, etc;
 Tensors backends, which enables the main  Nx API to be used to manipulate binary tensors, GPU-backed tensors, sparse matrices, and more;
 Numerical definitions, known as  defn, provide multi-stage compilation of tensor operations to multiple targets, such as highly specialized CPU code or the GPU. The compilation can happen either ahead-of-time (AOT) or just-in-time (JIT) with a compiler of your choice;
 You can find planned enhancements and features in the issues tracker. If you need one particular feature to move forward, don't hesitate to let us know and give us feedback.
 Nx's mascot is the Numbat, a marsupial native to southern Australia. Unfortunately the Numbat are endangered and it is estimated to be fewer than 1000 left. If you enjoy this project, consider donating to Numbat conservation efforts, such as  Project Numbat and  Australian Wildlife Conservancy.
 For Python developers,  Nx currently takes its main inspirations from   Numpy and   JAX but packaged into a single unified library.
    Discussions about Nx are also welcome in any of the Elixir community spaces, such as the  Elixir Forum and the other channels listed  on the sidebar of elixir-lang.org.
  In order to use  Nx, you will need Elixir installed. Then create an Elixir project via the  mix build tool:
  Then you can add  Nx as dependency in your  mix.exs. At the moment you will have to use a Git dependency while we work on our first release:
      iex > t  =  Nx. tensor([[ 1,  2], [ 3,  4]])iex >  Nx. divide( Nx. exp(t),  Nx. sum( Nx. exp(t)))  #Nx.Tensor< f64[ 2][ 2] [ [ 0.03205860328008499,  0.08714431874203257], [ 0.23688281808991013,  0.6439142598879722] ] >
  By default,  Nx uses pure Elixir code. Since Elixir is a functional and immutable language, each operation above makes a copy of the tensor, which is quite innefficient.
 However,  Nx also comes with numerical definitions, called  defn, which is a subset of Elixir tailored for numerical computations. For example, it overrides Elixir's default operators so they are tensor-aware:
  defn supports multiple compiler backends, which can compile said functions to run on the CPU or in the GPU. For example,  using the  EXLA compiler, which provides bindings to Google's XLA:
  Once  softmax is called,  Nx.Defn will invoke  EXLA to emit a just-in-time and high-specialized compiled version of the code, tailored to the tensor type and shape. By passing  client: :cuda or  client: :rocm, the code can be compiled for the GPU. For reference, here are some benchmarks of the function above when called with a tensor of one million random float values:
 Name ips average deviation median 99th %xla gpu f32 keep 15308.14 0.0653 ms ±29.01% 0.0638 ms 0.0758 msxla gpu f64 keep 4550.59 0.22 ms ±7.54% 0.22 ms 0.33 msxla cpu f32 434.21 2.30 ms ±7.04% 2.26 ms 2.69 msxla gpu f32 398.45 2.51 ms ±2.28% 2.50 ms 2.69 msxla gpu f64 190.27 5.26 ms ±2.16% 5.23 ms 5.56 msxla cpu f64 168.25 5.94 ms ±5.64% 5.88 ms 7.35 mselixir f32 3.22 311.01 ms ±1.88% 309.69 ms 340.27 mselixir f64 3.11 321.70 ms ±1.44% 322.10 ms 328.98 msComparison:xla gpu f32 keep 15308.14xla gpu f64 keep 4550.59 - 3.36x slower +0.154 msxla cpu f32 434.21 - 35.26x slower +2.24 msxla gpu f32 398.45 - 38.42x slower +2.44 msxla gpu f64 190.27 - 80.46x slower +5.19 msxla cpu f64 168.25 - 90.98x slower +5.88 mselixir f32 3.22 - 4760.93x slower +310.94 mselixir f64 3.11 - 4924.56x slower +321.63 ms
  defn relies on a technique called multi-stage programming, which is built on top of Elixir functional and meta-prgramming capabilities: we transform Elixir code to emit an AST that is then transformed to run on the CPU/GPU. Ultimately, the  defn compiler is pluggable, which means developers can implement bindings for different tensor compiler technologies and choose the most appropriate one.
 Many of Elixir features are supported inside  defn, such as the pipe operator, aliases, conditionals, pattern-matching, and more. Other features such as loops and in-place updates are on the roadmap.  defn also support  transforms, which allows numerical definitions to be transformed at runtime. Automatic differentiation, via the  grad function, is one example of transforms.

要收听《纽约时报》等发行商的更多音频故事，请下载iPhone或Android版Audm。
 旧金山—罗伯特·范·温克尔（Robert Van Winkle），是著名的说唱歌手Vanilla Ice，上周在网上举行了庭审，有1000多名粉丝。
 在漫长的谈话中，范温克尔先生赞扬了1990年代乐队Bell Biv DeVoe的姿势，并在被问及与麦当娜的关系时表示异议。他针对房地产和生活提出了建议，他说：“您必须保护自己的幸福，才能保护自己的生活。”某一时刻，一位与会者用他的热门歌曲“ Ice Ice Baby”的无伴奏合唱版本在聚会上演唱了小夜曲。
 几个小时后，范温克尔先生承认，他需要在孩子的母亲生气之前离开。
 这是一种随心所欲且不可预测的事件，它发生在Clubhouse上，昼夜不停，这是一个拥有11个月历史的社交媒体应用程序，在科技和大众文化的品味制造者中迅速普及，并迅速成为了关于言论自由和政治的辩论。
  据Apptopia称，该应用程序使人们可以在音频聊天室中讨论不同的主题，仅在上个月，该应用程序已被下载了近四百万次。像埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk），艾未未（Ai Weiwei），林赛·罗韩（Lindsay Lohan）和罗杰·斯通（Roger Stone）这样的公众人物都加入了该组织，其无限制的对话也引起了中国的愤怒，上周中国禁止该俱乐部。
 在此过程中，Clubhouse引发了关于音频是否是下一波社交媒体浪潮的争论，将数字连接从文本，照片和视频转移到老式语音。每天，在成千上万个聊天室中，Clubhouse的用户都对天体物理学，地缘政治学，宝莱坞中的酷儿形象甚至宇宙诗歌等话题进行了自由的交谈。 
“这是社交网络工作方式的重大变化，”戴夫·莫林（Dave Morin）说。他十多年前创立了社交网络Path，并投资了Clubhouse。 “我相信这是一个新的篇章。”
 Clubhouse的发展轨迹非常迅速-5月只有数千名用户-尽管该应用程序仅受邀请使用并且并不广泛。邀请如此令人垂涎，以至于它们在eBay上的售价高达89美元。诸如Barstool Sports之类的媒体公司也已经建立了Clubhouse帐户，并且至少有一家公司表示计划雇用“ Clubhouse的高级主管”。
  这种关注使这家规模不大的旧金山初创公司不堪重负，该公司拥有大约十二名员工，由两位企业家Paul Davison和Rohan Seth创立。 Clubhouse上个月筹集了超过1亿美元的资金，价值10亿美元，但它一直在努力应对激增的流量。在星期三，该应用程序崩溃了。此外，Facebook和Twitter正在开发类似产品以与其竞争。
  俱乐部会所还对骚扰，误传信息和隐私的投诉不断增加。上个月的一次事件中，一位用户宣传了关于冠状病毒疫苗的阴谋论，并劝阻人们开枪，导致骚扰一名女医生。
 本月，德国和意大利监管机构公开质疑Clubhouse的数据惯例是否符合欧洲数据保护法。在该国严格的互联网控制范围之外，在该国引发了政治对话之后，中国封锁了该应用程序。
 俱乐部会所遵循的是一条经典的硅谷创业之路，Twitter，Snapchat和Facebook等社交媒体公司也纷纷涉足：病毒式增长，随之而来的是混乱的问题。这是多年来第一家爆发的美国社交媒体公司。上一次全球社交网络热潮是TikTok，这是一款中国人拥有的应用程序，可将15秒的视频投放到文化交流中。
 40岁的戴维森先生和36岁的塞思先生拒绝接受采访。在周日的俱乐部讨论中，戴维森表示，该公司正急于雇用，开发新功能并发布该应用的Android版本。 
他说：“这太疯狂了，我们加入了这么多人。”
 都曾参加斯坦福大学的戴维森先生和塞思先生都是重复创业者。 Davison先生创建了多个社交网络应用程序，包括Highlight，该应用程序使用户可以查看附近的人并向其发送消息。塞思先生曾是Google工程师，与他人共同创立了一家公司Memry Labs，该公司开发了应用程序。这些初创企业要么被收购，要么被关闭。
 在2019年，这两个人（于2011年在技术界相识）建立了原型播客应用“ Talkshow”，他们称之为“最后一次尝试”。戴维森上个月在接受“ Hello Monday”播客采访时说，Talkshow感觉太像是正式广播，因此他们决定为人们自发加入对话提供一种方法。
 去年三月，戴维森先生和塞思先生成立了Clubhouse。他们增加了一种方法，使多个扬声器可以同时广播，并允许人们在数字房间之间跳动，就像他们在音乐节或商务会议上从一个台到另一个台。为了避免给初创企业带来压力，他们缓慢地发出邀请。
  随着人们在流行中寻求新的联系方式，该应用程序大受欢迎。它最早的用户是硅谷风险投资家，例如马克·安德森（Marc Andreessen）和他的商业伙伴本·霍洛维茨（Ben Horowitz），他们将Clubhouse引入了他们的网络。奥普拉·温弗瑞（Oprah Winfrey），MC哈默（MC Hammer）和约翰·梅耶（John Mayer）也加入了。
 TechNexus Venture Collaborative的投资人安迪·安纳科内（Andy Annacone）说：“这种访问感觉很难复制。”该公司经营着一支投资于Clubhouse的基金。
 今年5月，安德森（Andreessen）先生和霍洛维茨（Horowitz）的风险投资公司安德森霍洛维茨（Andreessen Horowitz）向Clubhouse投入了1000万美元，使其市值达到1亿美元。当时有两名员工。 
TikTok的影响者，YouTube明星和“单身汉”的演员很快就活跃在该应用程序上。它还催生了自己的明星，“建议用户列表”中有一些人聚集了超过一百万的关注者。去年12月，Clubhouse推出了仅受邀请的“创作者试点计划”，以帮助所谓的高级用户在该应用上赚钱。
 “人们已经在树立品牌，”现年38岁的Better Tomorrow Ventures创始人Sheel Mohnot说，他在该应用上拥有120万关注者。 “所有这些会所表演。我看过的其中一些节目是赞助的。” （戴维森先生和塞思先生曾表示，该公司计划通过售票活动，订阅和小费赚钱，但不会出售广告。）
  这种增长伴随着批评，即有色妇女和有色人种经常成为虐待对象，有关反犹太主义，同性恋恐惧症，种族主义和厌女症的讨论也在增加。
 32岁的波莎·贝尔（Porsha Belle）是休斯敦的一家会所影响者，她说，在谈到应用程序上的厌女症后，人们形成了互相鼓励彼此举报她的帐户的房间，因此她将被禁止入内。她的帐户上周一已被暂停。
 她说，她曾试图向该公司提出上诉，但是却无济于事。她说：“当欺凌者自由漫游时，我的页面被暂停了。”
 40岁的聋人德克萨斯州奥斯汀的社交媒体经理Rachelle Dooley说，她被封锁了，被赶出了一些会所房间。
 她说：“我可以看到它显示在隐藏式字幕上，人们说，‘为什么这位聋哑女士在音频应用程序上？” “我会冻结并开始哭泣。” 
Clubhouse具有“阻止”功能，可让用户更好地控制自己的空间。有时，这反而引起了有关访问的争议，包括与《纽约时报》的一名记者。
 卡内基·梅隆大学（Carnegie Mellon University）的美国和非洲裔研究学者，现年48岁的金伯利·埃利斯（Kimberly Ellis）表示，她还曾在会所房间里，那里的人们似乎在分发财务建议，但却“在做多层营销”。
 她说：“有些人想教练并从中获得收益。”
 在周日的会所讨论中，戴维森表示，公司有明确的规定，禁止传播错误信息，仇恨言论，虐待和欺凌行为。这家初创公司去年表示，它将增加顾问和安全功能，并授权主持人。
 然而，Clubhouse还使在中国和土耳其等国家/地区受到严格审查的人们可以自由谈论许多话题。一些用户说他们被迷住了。
 洛杉矶品牌策略师33岁的布里埃尔·里奇（Brielle Riche）表示，自11月份开始使用Clubhouse以来，Clubhouse就已经开放了自己的世界。
 她说：“俱乐部会所为我们提供了与陌生人交往的机会。” “只有Clubhouse才能让您摆脱TikTok。” 
Clubhouse上个月宣布了最新资金的一周后，马斯克（Musk）出现在应用程序上并引起了狂热，并采访了股票交易应用程序Robinhood的首席执行官弗拉德·特内夫（Vlad Tenev）。 马斯克先生已承诺与坎耶·韦斯特一起返回俱乐部，并邀请俄罗斯总统弗拉基米尔·V·普京加入该应用程序。  几天后，Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）出现了关于虚拟现实和增强现实的话题。 然后中国禁止了该应用程序。  星期日，有5,000人参加了每周一次的“市政厅”会议，这是俱乐部会所中最多的人。 Davison先生加入时间较晚，因为他曾在另一个房间里欢迎弗吉尼亚州民主党参议员Tim Kaine使用该应用程序。
note_en:
To hear more audio stories from publishers like The New York Times,   download Audm for iPhone or Android .
 SAN FRANCISCO — Robert Van Winkle, who is better known as the rapper  Vanilla Ice, held court online last week with more than 1,000 fans.
 In a rambling conversation, Mr. Van Winkle praised the poses of the 1990s band Bell Biv DeVoe and demurred when asked about  his relationship with Madonna. He dispensed advice on real estate and life, saying, “You got to protect your happiness to protect your life.” At one point, an attendee serenaded the gathering with an a cappella version of his hit “Ice Ice Baby.”
 Several hours later, Mr. Van Winkle confessed that he needed to leave before the mother of his child got angry.
 It was the kind of freewheeling and unpredictable event that has been happening around the clock on Clubhouse, an 11-month-old social media app that has exploded in popularity with the tastemakers of tech and popular culture and that is quickly becoming a town square for debates over free speech and politics.
  The app, which lets people gather in audio chatrooms to discuss different topics, has been downloaded nearly four million times in the last month alone, according to Apptopia. Public figures as various as Elon Musk, Ai Weiwei, Lindsay Lohan and Roger Stone have joined it, and the unconstrained conversations it has enabled have incurred the wrath of China, which  banned Clubhouse last week.
 In the process, Clubhouse has generated debate about whether audio is the next wave of social media, moving digital connections beyond text, photos and videos to old-fashioned voice. In thousands of chatrooms every day, Clubhouse’s users have conducted unfettered conversations on subjects as varied as astrophysics, geopolitics, queer representation in Bollywood and even cosmic poetry.
 “This is a major change in how the social internet works,” said Dave Morin,  who founded the  social network Path more than a decade ago and has invested in Clubhouse. “I believe it’s a new chapter.”
 Clubhouse’s trajectory has been rapid — it had just a  few thousand users in May — even though the app is invitation-only and not widely available. The invitations are so coveted that they have been listed on eBay for as much as  $89. Media companies such as Barstool Sports have also set up Clubhouse accounts, and at least one firm has said it plans to hire a “ senior Clubhouse executive.”
  The attention has overwhelmed the tiny San Francisco start-up, which has around a dozen employees and was founded by two entrepreneurs, Paul Davison and Rohan Seth. While Clubhouse raised more than $100 million in funding last month and was valued at $1 billion, it has struggled to handle the surging traffic. On Wednesday, the app crashed. Also,  Facebook and Twitter are working on similar products to compete with it.
  Clubhouse is also contending with rising complaints about harassment, misinformation and privacy. In one  incident last month, a user promoted conspiracy theories about the coronavirus vaccines and discouraged people from getting the shots, leading to harassment of a female doctor.
 This month, German and Italian regulators  publicly questioned whether Clubhouse’s data practices complied with European data protection laws. And China blocked the app after political conversations popped up on it outside the country’s tight internet controls.
 Clubhouse is following a classic Silicon Valley start-up path that social media companies like Twitter, Snapchat and Facebook have also trod: viral growth followed by the messy issues that come with it. It is the first American social media company to break out in years. The last global social networking hit was  TikTok, a Chinese-owned app that catapulted 15-second videos into the cultural discourse.
 Mr. Davison, 40, and Mr. Seth, 36, declined to be interviewed. In a Clubhouse discussion on Sunday, Mr. Davison said the company was rushing to hire, build new features and release an Android version of the app.
 “It’s just been crazy, we’ve had so many people joining,” he said.
 Mr. Davison and Mr. Seth, who both attended Stanford University, are repeat entrepreneurs. Mr. Davison created several social networking apps, including Highlight, which allowed users to see and message people nearby. Mr. Seth was a Google engineer and co-founded a company, Memry Labs, which built apps. Those start-ups were either bought or shut down.
 In 2019, the two men — who had met through tech circles in 2011 — built a prototype podcasting app, Talkshow, which they  called their “one last try.” But Talkshow felt too much like a formal broadcast, so they decided to add a way for people to spontaneously join the conversation, Mr. Davison said in an interview with the “Hello Monday”  podcast last month.
 Last March, Mr. Davison and Mr. Seth started Clubhouse. They added a way for multiple speakers to broadcast at once and allowed people to bounce between digital rooms as if they were going from stage to stage at a music festival or business conference. To avoid overwhelming their start-up, they doled out invitations slowly.
  The app caught on as people sought new ways to connect with one another in the pandemic. Some of its earliest users were Silicon Valley venture capitalists such as  Marc Andreessen and his business partner  Ben Horowitz, who introduced Clubhouse to their networks. Oprah Winfrey, MC Hammer and John Mayer joined.
 “There’s this feeling of access that’s really hard to replicate,” said Andy Annacone, an investor at TechNexus Venture Collaborative, which operates a fund that invested in Clubhouse.
 In May, Mr. Andreessen and Mr. Horowitz’s venture firm, Andreessen Horowitz, put $10 million into Clubhouse, valuing it at $100 million. It had two employees at the time.
 TikTok influencers, YouTube stars and cast members of “The Bachelor” soon became active on the app. It also spawned its own stars, with some people on its “suggested user list” amassing more than one million followers. In December, Clubhouse unveiled an invitation-only “ creator pilot program” to help so-called power users make money on the app.
 “People are already building brands,” said Sheel Mohnot, 38, founder of Better Tomorrow Ventures, who has 1.2 million followers on the app. “There’s all these Clubhouse shows. Some of those shows I’ve seen are sponsored.” (Mr. Davison and Mr. Seth have said the company plans to make money through ticketed events, subscriptions and tipping, but will not sell ads.)
  The growth has been accompanied by criticism that women and people of color are  frequent targets of abuse and that discussions involving  anti-Semitism,  homophobia,  racism  and  misogyny are on the rise.
 Porsha Belle, 32, a Clubhouse influencer in Houston, said that after she spoke up about misogyny on the app, people formed rooms to encourage one another to report her account so she would be barred. Her account was suspended last Monday.
 She said she had tried appealing to the company, but found little recourse. “My page is suspended while the bullies get to roam free,” she said.
 Rachelle Dooley, 40, a social media manager in Austin, Texas, who is deaf, said she had been blocked and kicked out of some Clubhouse rooms.
 “I can see it show up on the closed caption, people saying, ‘Why is this deaf lady on an audio app?’” she said. “I’d freeze and start crying.”
 Clubhouse has a “blocking” feature to give users more control over their spaces. That has in turn sometimes created disputes about access, including with a New York Times journalist.
 Kimberly Ellis, 48, an American and Africana studies scholar at Carnegie Mellon University who leads workshops on digital safety, said she had also been in Clubhouse rooms where people appeared to dispense financial advice but were instead “doing multilevel marketing.”
 “Some want to coach you and get money from you for their courses,” she said.
 In Sunday’s Clubhouse discussion, Mr. Davison said the company has explicit rules against spreading misinformation, hate speech, abuse and bullying. The start-up  said last year that it was adding advisers and safety features and empowering moderators.
 Yet Clubhouse has also enabled  people living under strict censorship in countries such as China and Turkey to speak freely about many topics. Some users said they were hooked.
 Brielle Riche, 33, a brand strategist in Los Angeles, said Clubhouse had opened up her world since she started using it in November.
 “Clubhouse gives us the opportunity to connect with strangers,” she said. “Only Clubhouse can get you off TikTok.”
 A week after Clubhouse announced its newest funding last month, Mr. Musk set off a frenzy when he appeared on the app and interviewed  Vlad Tenev, the chief executive of the stock trading app Robinhood. Mr. Musk has promised to return to Clubhouse with Kanye West and has invited President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia to the app.
   A few days later, Mark Zuckerberg, Facebook’s chief executive, turned up to chat about virtual and augmented reality. Then China banned the app.
 On Sunday, 5,000 people — the maximum in a Clubhouse room — attended a weekly “town hall” session with the founders. Mr. Davison joined late because he had been in another room welcoming Senator Tim Kaine, Democrat of Virginia, to the app.
title: 会所，一个微小的音频聊天应用程序，突破了
title_en: Clubhouse, a Tiny Audio Chat App, Breaks Through

note:
一台苏联纳粹德国的Enigma密码机的售价是其拍卖要价的两倍多，而伪装成一包香烟的秘密照相机的价格则将近20,000美元。
  一台Fialka M-125-3M 10转子密码机及其附件，在周末举行的美国拍卖会上以22,400美元的价格售出，胜过该设备的8,000至12,000美元的估计售价。
    Fialka和苏联时代使用的许多其他监视对象都来自克格勃间谍博物馆。博物馆设在纽约，收集并分类了与苏联间谍部队，克格勃（Komitet Gosudarstvennoy Bezopasnosti）和其他物品有关的历史物品，直到2020年10月冠状病毒大流行将其杀死后，才首次开放。
  Fialka于1956年由加密博物馆（Crypto Museum）推出，是一台机电密码机，遵循与纳粹德国臭名昭著的Enigma装置相同的基本原理。转子将来自苏联（和东欧）指挥官的纯文本消息加扰成密文，以备传输。
      美国史密森尼博物馆（Smithsonian Museum）对短暂存在的以苏联为主题的竞争对手表示蔑视。该公司内部杂志上的一篇文章对博物馆似乎如何消除20世纪俄罗斯充满仇恨的残酷行为表示了哀叹，转而使用Instagram兼容的互动展品，其背景和标签比学术历史学家所享受的要少。
  值得一提的是，拍卖中包括的是The Thing的复制品-美国大玺的木制模型，于1945年赠予美国驻莫斯科大使馆，并放置在大使馆的桌子后面。 Thing是一个基于共振的麦克风和发射器，藏在海豹内部，被美国人发现并传送给MI5，以使他们了解其工作原理。
  “东西”带有俄罗斯人已投入使用的一件设备的标志，大概是为了确保在国务卿访问之前就已安装了该设备。前MI5和MI6人物Peter Wright在他的古老但高度可读的自传Spycatcher中写道。 
赖特（Wright）是MI5专家，他推断Thing的麦克风以800MHz的频率向附近的听筒发送了语音。  同时，在美国拍卖会上，一台KGB间谍相机伪装成一包John Player香烟卖到19200美元，比拍卖前的估计价格600-800美元高出许多倍。  读者可能会将所有这些高科技产品与20世纪中叶的模拟时代联系起来，那时消费级微处理器的出现以及世界向数字化生活方式和经商的转移。 在看《卫报》报纸上精选的拍卖要点的照片时，肯定会想到Inspector Gadget和James Bond。 ®  注册-技术社区的独立新闻和观点。 情境发布的一部分
note_en:
A Soviet equivalent of Nazi Germany's Enigma cipher machine has sold for more than double its auction asking price – while a secret camera disguised as a pack of cigarettes went for nearly $20,000.
  A Fialka M-125-3M 10-rotor cipher code machine complete with accessories sold for $22,400 at a US auction held over the weekend, trumping the device's $8,000-$12,000 estimated sale price.
    The Fialka, and many other surveillance objects used in the Soviet era, came from the KGB Espionage Museum's collection. Based in New York, the museum collected and catalogued historical items relating to the Soviet spy force, the KGB (Komitet Gosudarstvennoy Bezopasnosti), and other items – until the coronavirus pandemic killed it off in October 2020, barely a year after it first opened.
  Introduced in 1956, as related by the  Crypto Museum, the Fialka was an electromechanical cipher machine which followed the same basic principles as Nazi Germany's infamous Enigma device. Rotors scrambled plaintext messages from Soviet (and Eastern European) commanders into ciphertext ready for transmission.
      The US Smithsonian Museum  poured scorn on its short-lived Soviet-themed rival. An article in its in-house magazine lamented how the museum seemingly omitted the hate-filled brutality of 20th century Russia in favour of Instagrammable, interactive exhibits with less context and labelling than an academic historian might have enjoyed.
  Remarkably, included in the auction was  a replica of The Thing – a wooden mock-up of the Great Seal of the United States, gifted to the US for its Moscow embassy in 1945 and placed behind the ambassador's desk. The Thing was a resonance-based microphone and transmitter hidden inside the seal, discovered by the Americans and conveyed to MI5 for them to figure out how it worked.
  "The Thing bore the hallmarks of a piece of equipment which the Russians had rushed into service, presumably to ensure it was installed before the Secretary of State's visit," wrote former MI5 and MI6 man Peter Wright in his old-but-highly-readable autobiography  Spycatcher.
  Wright was the MI5 specialist who deduced that The Thing's microphone transmitted speech to a nearby listening post on 800MHz.
  Meanwhile, back at the US auction, a KGB spy camera disguised to look like a packet of John Player cigarettes  fetched $19,200 – many times greater than its pre-auction estimate of $600-$800.
    Readers will probably relate all these hi-tech gizmos back to the analogue age of the mid-20th century, before the advent of consumer-scale microprocessors and the move of the world to digital ways of living and doing business. Certainly there's something of Inspector Gadget and James Bond that comes to mind while looking at  The Guardian newspaper's  photo essay of selected auction highlights. ®
        The Register - Independent news and views for the tech community. Part of Situation Publishing
title: 苏联“谜”密码机在倒塌博物馆的拍卖会上以22,000美元的价格售出
title_en: Soviet 'Enigma' cipher machine sells for $22k at collapsed museum's auction
